---
title: "Masters Thesis"
subtitle: "California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo"
author: "Kyle Cahitas"
date: today
---

## [Your Thesis Title Here]

::: {.callout-note}
**Status**: In progress — expected completion June 2026  
**Advisor**: [Advisor Name] | Department of [Your Department]  
**Program**: M.S. Environmental Science, Cal Poly SLO
:::

## Abstract

*Full abstract pending. Brief description of the work:*

[2–3 sentences describing what your thesis investigates, 
the methods you're using, and the expected contribution. 
Write this like you'd explain it to a colleague in a 
different department.]

---

## Research Question

[One crisp sentence. What is the central question your 
thesis answers?]

## Study Area

[Brief description of your field site, watershed, or 
spatial extent. Add a map here when ready.]

---

## Methods Overview

[High-level description of your workflow. Bullet form is 
fine here — this is a summary, not a methods section.]

- **Data sources**: 
- **Primary tools**: R, [packages], QGIS
- **Analytical approach**: 

---

## Code Snippets

*Selected analytical methods from the thesis workflow.*

### [Method 1 — e.g., Data Processing]
```{{r}}
#| label: fig-example
#| fig-cap: "Caption describing what this shows"
#| echo: true

# Paste a representative chunk of your actual R code here
# Keep it to ~20-30 lines — enough to show methodology
# without exposing your full analysis

library(tidyverse)
library(sf)

# example placeholder
# your_data |>
#   filter(...) |>
#   ggplot(aes(...)) +
#   geom_...() +
#   theme_minimal()
```

### [Method 2 — e.g., Spatial Analysis]
```{{r}}
#| echo: true

# Second representative chunk
# Focus on showing something visually interesting
# or methodologically distinct from chunk 1
```

---

## Preliminary Results

::: {.callout-warning}
Results are preliminary and subject to change prior to final submission.
:::

[2–3 sentences describing what you're finding so far. 
Vague is fine — "initial results suggest X relationship 
between Y and Z." This signals progress without oversharing 
unpublished work.]

### Key Figure

[Drop your most compelling figure here when ready.
Even a preliminary plot is better than nothing.]


---

## Related Work

- [Link to any related conference poster, presentation, or preprint if applicable]
- Thesis repository: *private until defense, available upon request*

---

*This page will be updated as the thesis progresses. 
Full document available after defense — June 2026.*
